mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS trend lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because falling rates are then interpreted as policy easing rather than recession stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS falls over the prior 3 months and CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or decelerating, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent local low signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, causing earnings expectations to compress and pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and GDPC1 both accelerate, nominal spending and real output are expanding together, indicating strong aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in RSAFS and GDPC1 does not beat either series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline in the top quartile of the joint-growth signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in INDPRO indicates supply-side contraction and margin pressure, which increases recession odds and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly INDPRO declines beyond the threshold are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior 3-month SPX trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in HOUST signals collapsing residential demand and tighter credit transmission, which leads equity investors to mark down growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if sustained below-trend HOUST readings do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns with at least 55% hit rate, or if the signal has no explanatory power in a rolling 4-year evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll growth slowdown combined with an unemployment rise identifies a deteriorating labor market that weakens consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not outperform either single series in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered episodes do not show a negative average excess return.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS combined with elevated CPIAUCSL compresses real disposable demand and valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative when inflation remains above trend.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns at a rate above 55%, or if the signal flips positive during sustained tightening without a corresponding decline in CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 and PAYEMS together indicate reacceleration in output and hiring, which supports risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1 and PAYEMS acceleration fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in the majority of observations, or if the joint signal is not materially better than the unconditional baseline.
